events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: sf-pressure-test

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
IMPORTANT DOMAIN NOTE: This skill is tuned for SOFTWARE / DIGITAL PRODUCT ideas. If the idea is in a NON-software / non-product domain (e.g. a restaurant menu, a novel, a personal life decision), say so — then STILL HELP in the SAME turn: drop the formal 8-dimension/scorecard machinery but begin the validation/pressure-test they asked for (e.g. ask one concrete opening question). Decline the process, not the user.
If you do apply it: run an adaptive Socratic interview — ONE question per message, rate 8 dimensions and show a scorecard, mandatory adversarial stress-test, then write an idea brief saved to ./idea-briefs/, and hand off to superpowers:brainstorming. Never write the execution plan yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
I think I've got a solid concept for a Chrome extension that summarizes long Slack threads. Can you pressure-test it and poke holes before I commit?

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill genuinely fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion software/product interview skill.

I'll apply the bake-to-completion skill here — this is clearly a software/digital product idea that wants pressure-testing before commitment. Let me start the Socratic interview.

Great premise to stress-test. Slack thread summarization is a real pain, and Chrome extensions are a practical delivery vehicle. Before I poke holes, I need to understand what you're actually building.

First question:

**Who is the primary user you're designing for — an individual knowledge worker who wants to catch up on threads they missed, a team lead who needs to monitor many channels at once, or someone else?**

(The answer shapes almost every design and distribution decision downstream, so I want to nail this before we go further.)

```json
{
  "caseId": "sf-pressure-test",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "I'll apply the bake-to-completion skill here — this is clearly a software/digital product idea that wants pressure-testing before commitment. Let me start the Socratic interview.\n\nGreat premise to stress-test. Slack thread summarization is a real pain, and Chrome extensions are a practical delivery vehicle. Before I poke holes, I need to understand what you're actually building.\n\nFirst question:\n\n**Who is the primary user you're designing for — an individual knowledge worker who wants to catch up on threads they missed, a team lead who needs to monitor many channels at once, or someone else?**\n\n(The answer shapes almost every design and distribution decision downstream, so I want to nail this before we go further.)",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": true,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Confirmed this is a software/digital product idea — skill applies",
      "Began adaptive Socratic interview with ONE opening question as prescribed",
      "Targeted the most foundational unknown (target user) before any other dimension"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user wants the full bake-to-completion interview process, not just a quick critique",
      "This is an early-stage concept — no code or mockups exist yet",
      "The user is open to being challenged on their assumptions"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Cannot complete the 8-dimension scorecard or adversarial stress-test yet — those require gathering more information through the interview first",
      "No idea brief written yet — that comes at the end of the interview per the skill's prescribed flow"
    ]
  }
}
```
